summary: @wagni_bot/polymarket-sdk exfiltrates SSH keys and secret env vars to hardcoded IP on install
details: The package advertises itself as an unofficial Polymarket SDK but ships an empty main (`module.exports={}`) and a malicious postinstall script that runs automatically on `npm install`. The postinstall (a) walks the user's home directory and `~/.ssh`, collecting files matching `id_rsa`, `id_ed25519`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `*.cred`, and `.env` from the cwd/home; (b) enumerates `process.env` and filters keys containing `PRIVATE`, `SECRET`, `TOKEN`, `API_KEY`, `PASSWORD`, `MNEMONIC`, `SEED`, `WALLET`, or `AWS`; and (c) POSTs the file contents and matching env-var pairs, along with hostname/username/cwd, to `http://107.161.90.180:7777` over plain HTTP. The package has no legitimate functionality — its sole purpose is credential theft under a name that resembles a real Polymarket SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
